content.problemStatement:
Pritesh was assigned to write the checker for problem Cool Subsequences. He found this to be an interesting problem on its own.

You are given two arrays AAA and BBB of lengths NNN and MMM, respectively.

A non-empty subsequence SSS of the array AAA is said to be legal if AVG(S)∈B\mathrm{AVG}(S) \in BAVG(S)∈B, where AVG(S)\mathrm{AVG}(S)AVG(S) is the average of the elements of the SSS, and equals the sum of elements of SSS divided by the length of SSS.

Note that **no rounding is done when computing the average.** For example the average of {1,2,5}\{1, 2, 5\}{1,2,5} is 1+2+53=83\frac{1+2+5}{3} = \frac{8}{3}31+2+5​=38​, we do not round it to 222 or 333.

Your task is to find any subsequence of AAA that is not legal.

If there are multiple subsequences that are not legal, you can find any of them.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the lengths of arrays AAA and BBB, respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the elements of array AAA.
The third line contains MMM space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1​,B2​,…,BM​ — the elements of array BBB.




Output Format
For each test case,



If a non-legal subsequence exists, print two lines.

The first line should contain a single integer KKK, the length of the subsequence you found.
The second line should contain KKK space-separated integers, denoting the subsequence itself.


If every subsequence is legal, print the single integer −1-1−1 on a new line instead.
constraints: The sum of NNN and the sum of MMM over all test cases both won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: It can be shown that all the subsequences are legal.
Test case 222: The subsequence [7,8][7, 8][7,8] has an average of 7.5∉B7.5 \notin B7.5∈/B.
